User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the CityFix application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

Report.title: Report
Report.description: Represents a report of an infrastructure problem.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Report entity.

description.description: Detailed description of the infrastructure problem.

latitude.description: Latitude of the infrastructure problem location.

longitude.description: Longitude of the infrastructure problem location.

category.description: Category of the infrastructure problem (e.g., potholes, broken streetlights).

summary.description: AI generated summary of the problem reported.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. Uses path-based ownership for private user data.
Stores reports submitted by users. Path-based ownership is used to link reports to the user who created them.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support the CityFix application, focusing on user management and infrastructure problem reporting. It prioritizes authorization independence by using path-based ownership for user data and reports. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules, enhancing security and scalability.

The structure segregates user data and reports into separate collections under the `/users/{userId}` path. This structural segregation ensures that each collection has a homogeneous security posture, simplifying security rule management.

The design supports required QAPs (Queries are not Filters) by clearly defining ownership via path structure.
